studies.officialTitle: A Study of Tirzepatide in Participants With Heart Failure With Preserved Ejection Fraction and Obesity

design.description: Event-driven parallel-group trial with a maximum tolerated tirzepatide dose.

studies.population: Adults with heart failure with preserved ejection fraction and obesity.
studies.overallDuration: At least 52 weeks; median follow-up 104 weeks
studies.safetySummary: Adverse events leading to treatment discontinuation occurred more frequently with tirzepatide and were mainly gastrointestinal.

sources.title: Tirzepatide for Heart Failure with Preserved Ejection Fraction and Obesity

studies.officialTitle: A Study of Tirzepatide (LY3298176) in Participants With Obesity or Overweight

design.description: Parallel-group fixed target-dose trial with lifestyle intervention.

studies.population: Adults with obesity, or overweight with at least one weight-related complication, without diabetes.
studies.safetySummary: Gastrointestinal adverse events were the most common and were mainly mild to moderate; treatment discontinuation increased with dose.

sources.title: Tirzepatide Once Weekly for the Treatment of Obesity

studies.officialTitle: A Study of Tirzepatide (LY3298176) in Participants With Obesity or Overweight and Type 2 Diabetes

design.description: Parallel-group target-dose trial with lifestyle intervention.

studies.population: Adults with obesity or overweight and type 2 diabetes; weight management was an explicit objective.
studies.safetySummary: Gastrointestinal adverse events were most common, were generally mild to moderate, and occurred primarily during dose escalation.

sources.title: Tirzepatide once weekly for the treatment of obesity in people with type 2 diabetes

studies.officialTitle: A Study of Tirzepatide (LY3298176) in Participants With Obesity or Overweight Following Intensive Lifestyle Intervention

design.description: A 12-week intensive-lifestyle lead-in preceded the 72-week randomized period.

studies.population: Adults with obesity or overweight who achieved at least 5% weight loss during an intensive lifestyle lead-in, without diabetes.
studies.safetySummary: Gastrointestinal adverse events were the most common and were primarily mild to moderate.

sources.title: Tirzepatide after intensive lifestyle intervention in adults with overweight or obesity

studies.officialTitle: A Study of Tirzepatide (LY3298176) in Participants With Obesity or Overweight for the Maintenance of Weight Loss

design.description: After a 36-week open-label tirzepatide lead-in, participants were randomized to continue maximum tolerated dose or switch to placebo for 52 weeks.

studies.population: Adults with obesity or overweight and a weight-related complication, without diabetes, who completed the tirzepatide lead-in.
studies.safetySummary: Gastrointestinal adverse events were most common and were largely mild to moderate.

sources.title: Continued Treatment With Tirzepatide for Maintenance of Weight Reduction in Adults With Obesity

studies.officialTitle: A Study of Tirzepatide Compared With Semaglutide in Participants With Obesity or Overweight

design.description: Active-comparator parallel-group trial using maximum tolerated doses.

studies.population: Adults with obesity, or overweight with at least one weight-related complication, without diabetes.
studies.safetySummary: Gastrointestinal adverse events were most common in both groups and were mainly mild to moderate during dose escalation.

sources.title: Tirzepatide as Compared with Semaglutide for the Treatment of Obesity

studies.officialTitle: A Study of Tirzepatide in Chinese Participants With Obesity or Overweight

design.description: Parallel-group target-dose trial conducted in China.

studies.population: Chinese adults with obesity or overweight, without diabetes.
studies.safetySummary: The most frequent adverse events were gastrointestinal and were mostly mild to moderate.

sources.title: Tirzepatide for weight reduction in Chinese adults with obesity

studies.officialTitle: A Study of Tirzepatide for Maintenance of Body Weight Reduction in Participants With Obesity or Overweight

design.description: After a 60-week maximum-tolerated-dose lead-in, participants were randomized to continue that dose, reduce to 5 mg, or switch to placebo for 52 weeks.

studies.population: Adults with obesity or overweight who completed 60 weeks of tirzepatide treatment.
studies.safetySummary: The safety profile was consistent with prior tirzepatide studies; gastrointestinal events were the most common adverse events.

sources.title: Tirzepatide for maintenance of bodyweight reduction in adults with obesity

studies.officialTitle: A Study of Mazdutide in Chinese Adults With Obesity or Overweight

design.description: Parallel-group dose-ranging trial conducted in China.

studies.population: Chinese adults with obesity, or overweight with at least one weight-related comorbidity, without diabetes.
studies.safetySummary: Gastrointestinal adverse events were the most common and were generally mild to moderate; the safety profile was consistent with the known pharmacology.

sources.title: Once-Weekly Mazdutide in Chinese Adults with Obesity or Overweight

studies.officialTitle: A Study of Mazdutide in Chinese Adults With Obesity

design.description: Parallel-group trial of high-dose mazdutide conducted in China.

studies.population: Chinese adults with obesity, with or without type 2 diabetes.
studies.safetySummary: Vomiting, nausea, and diarrhea were the most common adverse events; adverse-event discontinuation was uncommon but higher with mazdutide than placebo.

sources.title: Treatment With 9-mg Mazdutide for Weight Reduction in Chinese Adults With Obesity

studies.officialTitle: A Study of Retatrutide (LY3437943) in Participants Who Have Obesity or Are Overweight

design.description: Parallel-group dose-ranging trial with multiple starting-dose titration schedules.

studies.population: Adults with obesity, or overweight with at least one weight-related condition, without diabetes.
studies.safetySummary: The most common adverse events were gastrointestinal, were dose related and mostly mild to moderate; heart-rate increases peaked at 24 weeks and then declined.

sources.title: Triple-Hormone-Receptor Agonist Retatrutide for Obesity — A Phase 2 Trial

studies.officialTitle: A Study of Retatrutide (LY3437943) in Participants With Obesity or Overweight and Knee Osteoarthritis

design.description: Parallel-group trial evaluating weight and knee-osteoarthritis outcomes.

studies.population: Adults with obesity or overweight and knee osteoarthritis, without type 2 diabetes.
studies.safetySummary: The most common adverse events were gastrointestinal and were generally mild to moderate; discontinuation because of adverse events increased with dose.

studies.officialTitle: A Study of Orforglipron (LY3502970) in Participants With Obesity or Overweight With Weight-Related Comorbidities

design.description: Parallel-group oral-dose trial with lifestyle intervention.

studies.population: Adults with obesity, or overweight with at least one weight-related comorbidity, without diabetes.
studies.safetySummary: Gastrointestinal adverse events were the most common, were mainly mild to moderate, and treatment discontinuation increased with dose.

sources.title: Orforglipron for the Treatment of Obesity

studies.officialTitle: A Study of Orforglipron in Participants With Obesity or Overweight and Type 2 Diabetes

design.description: Parallel-group oral-dose trial with lifestyle intervention.

studies.population: Adults with obesity or overweight and type 2 diabetes; weight management was an explicit objective.
studies.safetySummary: The safety profile was consistent with the GLP-1 receptor agonist class, with gastrointestinal adverse events most frequent.

sources.title: Orforglipron in Adults with Obesity and Type 2 Diabetes

studies.officialTitle: A Study of Eloralintide (LY3841136) in Participants With Obesity or Overweight

design.description: Parallel-group dose-ranging trial including fixed-dose and dose-escalation regimens.

studies.population: Adults with obesity, or overweight with at least one weight-related condition, without diabetes.
studies.safetySummary: Nausea and fatigue were the most common adverse events; the study reported no new safety signal that changed the overall benefit-risk interpretation.

sources.title: Eloralintide, a Selective Amylin Receptor Agonist, for Obesity

studies.officialTitle: A Study of Multiple Doses of NNC0487-0111 in People With Overweight or Obesity

design.description: First-in-human multiple-ascending-dose study.

studies.population: Adults with overweight or obesity without diabetes.
studies.safetySummary: All treatment-emergent adverse events were mild or moderate; gastrointestinal symptoms and decreased appetite were most common and dose related.

studies.officialTitle: A Study of Subcutaneous Amycretin in People With Overweight or Obesity

design.description: Multiple-ascending-dose, dose-response study.

studies.population: Adults aged 18-55 years with overweight or obesity.
studies.safetySummary: A high frequency of gastrointestinal events was reported, consistent with GLP-1 and amylin agonism; no unexpected safety signal was disclosed.

studies.officialTitle: Investigation of Safety and Efficacy of Cagrilintide in Adults With Overweight or Obesity

design.masking: Double-blind for active treatment versus pooled placebo
design.description: Dose-ranging active- and placebo-controlled trial.

studies.population: Adults with overweight or obesity.
studies.safetySummary: Gastrointestinal disorders occurred in 41%-63% across cagrilintide doses versus 32% with placebo; nausea was the most frequent event.

studies.officialTitle: A Research Study to See How Well CagriSema Helps People With Excess Body Weight Lose Weight

design.description: Four-arm parallel-group trial with lifestyle intervention.

studies.population: Adults with obesity, or overweight with at least one weight-related complication, without diabetes.
studies.safetySummary: Gastrointestinal adverse events were the most common; treatment discontinuation rates were low across groups.

sources.title: Cagrilintide-Semaglutide in Adults with Overweight or Obesity

studies.officialTitle: A Research Study to See How Well CagriSema Helps People With Type 2 Diabetes and Excess Body Weight Lose Weight

design.description: Parallel-group 3:1 trial with lifestyle intervention.

studies.population: Adults with obesity or overweight and type 2 diabetes; body-weight reduction was a primary objective.
studies.safetySummary: The most common adverse events were gastrointestinal and were mainly mild to moderate.

sources.title: Cagrilintide-Semaglutide in Adults with Overweight or Obesity and Type 2 Diabetes

studies.officialTitle: A Research Study Comparing CagriSema With Tirzepatide in People With Obesity

design.description: Active-controlled head-to-head parallel-group trial.

studies.population: Adults with obesity without diabetes.
studies.safetySummary: Both treatments had safety profiles consistent with their component classes; full peer-reviewed safety results were not yet available.

studies.officialTitle: Effect of Liraglutide for Weight Management in Subjects With Type 2 Diabetes

design.description: Parallel-group 2:1:1 weight-management trial.

studies.population: Adults with obesity or overweight and type 2 diabetes; weight management was the explicit objective.
studies.safetySummary: Gastrointestinal disorders were more common with liraglutide than placebo; no pancreatitis was reported.

sources.title: Efficacy of Liraglutide for Weight Loss Among Patients With Type 2 Diabetes

studies.officialTitle: A Research Study on How Well Liraglutide Works in Children Living With Obesity

design.description: Parallel-group 2:1 trial with lifestyle intervention.

studies.population: Children aged 6 to under 12 years with nonmonogenic, nonsyndromic obesity.
studies.safetySummary: Adverse events occurred in 89% with liraglutide and 88% with placebo; gastrointestinal events occurred in 80% and 54%, respectively.

sources.title: Liraglutide for Children 6 to Under 12 Years of Age with Obesity

studies.officialTitle: Effect of Liraglutide in the Maintenance of Weight Loss

design.description: Parallel-group 2:1 trial with lifestyle modification.

studies.population: Adults with obesity, or overweight plus treated or untreated dyslipidemia or hypertension, without type 2 diabetes.
studies.safetySummary: Mild or moderate nausea and diarrhea were most frequent; serious adverse events occurred in 6.2% with liraglutide and 5.0% with placebo.

sources.title: SCALE Obesity and Prediabetes study record
A Randomized, Controlled Trial of 3.0 mg of Liraglutide in Weight Management

studies.officialTitle: A Long Term Study of Liraglutide in Adolescents Who Have Obesity

design.description: Parallel-group 1:1 trial with lifestyle therapy.

studies.population: Adolescents aged 12 to under 18 years with obesity and poor response to lifestyle therapy alone.
studies.safetySummary: Gastrointestinal adverse events occurred in 64.8% with liraglutide and 36.5% with placebo; discontinuations due to adverse events occurred in 10.4% and 0%.

sources.title: A Randomized, Controlled Trial of Liraglutide for Adolescents with Obesity

studies.officialTitle: A Research Study to See How Well Oral Semaglutide Helps People With Overweight or Obesity Lose Weight

design.description: Parallel-group trial with lifestyle intervention.

studies.population: Adults with obesity, or overweight with at least one weight-related comorbidity, without diabetes.
studies.safetySummary: Gastrointestinal adverse events were more frequent with oral semaglutide and were mostly mild to moderate.

sources.title: Oral Semaglutide at a Dose of 25 mg in Adults with Overweight or Obesity

studies.officialTitle: STEP 1: Research Study Investigating How Well Semaglutide Works in People Suffering From Overweight or Obesity

design.description: Parallel-group 2:1 trial with lifestyle intervention.

studies.population: Adults with obesity, or overweight with at least one weight-related comorbidity, without diabetes.
studies.safetySummary: Gastrointestinal disorders were the most frequent adverse events and were generally mild to moderate; nausea and diarrhea were usually transient.

sources.title: Once-Weekly Semaglutide in Adults with Overweight or Obesity

studies.officialTitle: Research Study Investigating How Well Semaglutide Works in People With Type 2 Diabetes Suffering From Overweight or Obesity

design.description: Parallel-group 1:1:1 trial with lifestyle intervention.

studies.population: Adults with obesity or overweight and type 2 diabetes; weight management was an explicit objective.
studies.safetySummary: Gastrointestinal adverse events were most frequent and occurred more often with semaglutide 2.4 mg than placebo.

sources.title: Semaglutide 2.4 mg once a week in adults with overweight or obesity, and type 2 diabetes

studies.officialTitle: Two-year Research Study Investigating How Well Semaglutide Works in People Suffering From Overweight or Obesity

design.description: Two-year parallel-group trial with behavioral intervention.

studies.population: Adults with obesity, or overweight with at least one weight-related comorbidity, without diabetes.
studies.safetySummary: Gastrointestinal adverse events, mostly mild to moderate, occurred in 82.2% with semaglutide and 53.9% with placebo.

sources.title: Two-year effects of semaglutide in adults with overweight or obesity

studies.officialTitle: A Research Study on How Well Semaglutide Works in Adolescents With Overweight or Obesity

design.description: Parallel-group 2:1 trial with lifestyle intervention.

studies.population: Adolescents aged 12 to under 18 years with obesity, or overweight plus a weight-related condition.
studies.safetySummary: Gastrointestinal events occurred in 62% with semaglutide and 42% with placebo; cholelithiasis occurred in five semaglutide participants and none receiving placebo.

sources.title: Once-Weekly Semaglutide in Adolescents with Obesity

studies.officialTitle: A Research Study to See How Well Semaglutide Helps People With Excess Body Weight Lose Weight

design.description: Parallel-group 5:1:1 trial with lifestyle intervention.

studies.population: Adults with obesity without diabetes.
studies.safetySummary: Gastrointestinal events occurred in 70.8%, 61.2%, and 42.8% with semaglutide 7.2 mg, 2.4 mg, and placebo; dysaesthesia was more frequent at 7.2 mg.

sources.title: Once-weekly semaglutide 7.2 mg in adults with obesity

studies.officialTitle: A Research Study to See How Well Semaglutide Helps People With Type 2 Diabetes and Excess Body Weight Lose Weight

design.description: Parallel-group 3:1:1 trial with lifestyle intervention.

studies.population: Adults with obesity and type 2 diabetes; weight management was an explicit objective.
studies.safetySummary: The safety profile was consistent with the GLP-1 receptor agonist class; gastrointestinal adverse events were the most common.

sources.title: Once-weekly semaglutide 7.2 mg in adults with obesity and type 2 diabetes

studies.officialTitle: A Study to Evaluate UBT251 in Chinese Adults With Overweight or Obesity

design.description: Four-arm dose-ranging trial.

studies.population: Chinese adults with overweight or obesity.
studies.safetySummary: The sponsor reported a safety profile consistent with the mechanism; detailed peer-reviewed safety results were not yet available.
